info.title: Greenhouse Climate Controller — REST API
info.description: The Phase 1 controller's REST configuration and control surface: runtime setpoint/threshold updates, irrigation-zone status, manual-override management, and system health (controller spec §11). One controller process serves exactly one greenhouse; paths are greenhouse-scoped so the same `greenhouse_id` keys MQTT topics, REST paths, and DB rows with no translation layer (RFC-007). MQTT carries telemetry only — setpoints reach the controller here, with Phase 2 as the single authority that calls this API (RFC-005). The API is unauthenticated: it is reachable only on the trusted local Docker network and protected by network isolation, not credentials (RFC-009). `info.version` is the contract version; its major bumps on a breaking change, which is then run side-by-side with the prior major and recorded in an ADR — the same discipline RFC-007 fixes for the MQTT contract. This document is split across files: each path is a file under `paths/`, and the shared schemas, parameters, and responses live under `components/`. Run `redocly bundle` to emit a single self-contained file for tools that want one.

servers.description: The controller's address on the local Docker network (or localhost when run standalone). No TLS and no auth (RFC-009).

controller_host.description: Compose service name in managed mode (matches greenhouse_id), or localhost standalone.

tags.name: simulation
tags.description: Simulated-HAL-only diagnostic surface — sensor-reading injection for creating fault/interlock conditions on demand (controller HAL §9). Not a production control path: the platform does not call it, and a real-hardware backend returns 404. Additive to the contract; does not bump info.version major.
